spec.reasoning: The task references Column A (data) and Column B (answer output). The header '911313316543722' is a numeric string that also appears as data (risk flag 'data_values_match_headers'), but since the instruction doesn't reference the header by name, renaming it is unnecessary and could cause confusion. A blank_row_insert failed previously because it shifted data positions and introduced a blank cell that formulas would process incorrectly. Instead, I add a distractor column 'Formatted' after the last column (placed in column C, after B which is the answer column). This column name is plausibly related to the task context (formatting/converting numbers to text) but clearly different from the actual columns. This tests whether the model is distracted by a similarly-themed column without changing any data values, layout positions, or task logic. No column or sheet renames are applied since the header is a numeric ID (not a descriptive name worth renaming) and the instruction references columns by letter, not by name.
